description: This is the day Alhea and I decided to part ways, but not on bad terms. It was to give her space, to heal her wounds, and to move forward from her previous relationship. We made a promise, a pinky promise, that during this time apart we will not be in any relationship, will not entertain anyone else, and will heal fully from the past. I will wait for her. I don't know until when, but I will wait. And I hope that when we meet again, she will be willing to accept my love again. We said our goodbyes at the terminal, but we spent some time together talking before we finally parted ways.

I was too early this day, around 8am I was already in the office, and I was so stressed because I accidentally deleted the SonicWall DHCP server lease. The first half of my shift was basically just manually adding back all the deleted IPs. My coworkers noticed I wasn't myself. I don't know, maybe I was still sad about what happened yesterday. Then in the afternoon, Alhea heart reacted to my message from the night before. I did some support work for other departments and made some papansin moves towards Alhea. I clocked out at 5pm and while driving home, Ms. Celia called needing assistance to review CCTV footage. I replied to her the moment I got home. Also that day I called Alhea and told her that I withdrew my resignation and we talked about what happened that day, but looking back that wasn't the right move. I started questioning myself. I should not have done that because I was supposed to be giving her space, and calling her was quite contradicting that.

Today is Sir Say's birthday. I submitted my cancellation letter to Ma'am Jen and she approved it, and I kept it from Sir Dennis. I want to see him sad until April 10. Haha. I did some papansin moves again like greeting her and her coworkers in the department. Before Sir Say's celebration, during the assembly, Ma'am Jen randomly called me out because I was sitting in front. I moved to the back where Alhea was also sitting, but we didn't talk or anything. Haha. After the event, I waved at her through the window where I could see Alhea. I don't know if she noticed it, but I waved at her anyway and left the office.

I came in at 9am today, about 10 minutes early for my 9am schedule. For the first half of the day I didn't do much, just answering calls and providing support to the stations and the office. Alhea and I met in the pantry but like before, we didn't talk or acknowledge each other. In the afternoon, Alhea came to our office to deliver an item from a supplier and again we didn't talk or acknowledge each other. I had a meeting with HR from 4:30pm to 7:30pm, just the usual workload. Alhea and I had a small talk. I said 'Di ka pa uuwi? Oras na.' and she also said 'Ikaw, di ka pa uuwi?' and I replied 'May meeting kami with HR, dami pinapagawa.' Oh and I almost forgot, I met her in my dreams. I don't know how but we were in a room, a yellow room, me, Alhea, and Gilbert. I don't know what we were doing but as far as I can remember, I was talking to Alhea.

Today was the celebration of Women's Month in the office. This was also the day I searched for a brooch pin lock for Alhea. I looked in Sta. Lucia and Facebook Marketplace but found no pin. Later that afternoon, I tried Ayala Feliz. I went there and found one, though they were not sold as separate pins. I needed to buy from an existing brooch. It was a bit sad but I made it work. I bought some and gave them to Alhea, but she said she had also lost the brooch itself. I was quite sad about that, but thankfully she found it because her niece had taken it from her lace. On the same day, Alhea joined the games at the Women's Month event held in our office. They didn't win though, I think. HR also asked me if I could take photos of them. I took some but they didn't turn out well, too dark. Also on this day, Ma'am Kim and I exchanged some thoughts about Alhea. Like it's okay to approach her, but you need to ask permission first. If she says yes, great. If she says no, move forward. But the idea is to make her feel that you still care and are reaching out.

Alhea and I met at the stairs. I was coming from the 2nd floor going down to my office, barely having timed in at that point. To my surprise, I bumped into her at the stairs, saying good morning and exchanging greetings. During lunch, I sat with them and she gave me leftovers from her lunch, an egg that she made herself. Later that day, I asked her if I could walk with her and she said yes. We walked and talked like before, just friendly interactions. We walked from the office all the way to Marikina Bayan out of nowhere. She just felt like walking so I accompanied her. It was fun hehe. I called her later that night and kept her up. Sorry!

I was late this time, far from my usual time in, but I didn't time in. So I sat there and when break time came at 10, I messaged her if I could get her coffee because she was sleepy and hadn't slept well that night because of me. She accepted my coffee and later that afternoon, I asked her again if I could walk with her. I also gave her strawberries from Sir Zhem and we walked again from the office to bayan. We made a stopover to eat goto because she was not feeling well today. She had a cold because of the hot weather and the cold air in the office. I tried to call her but she didn't answer. Maybe she was resting because she was not feeling well and I didn't want to bother her. I just wanted her to recover faster.

Alhea was absent in the morning. I asked her supervisor if she was going to come. She said no, she has a cold but I expected it already since yesterday she was not in good shape. Though her supervisor said she will be in for a half day, and I verified it through Telegram. I messaged her if she was going to the office today, she said yes and later in the afternoon. I checked up on her about her status, how she felt, if she was feeling sick, told her to take her meds, eat lunch and such. Everything went well today and we walked together towards the terminal. We talked and I shared some chismis I got from Kyla. I could tell she was not in good shape. She rode Jeep Padilla only and she said it was fine, she will ride again. I waved at her and said goodbye, she waved at me too. I also noticed that someone with a familiar energy rode the same jeep. I didn't know if it was her ex or not. I didn't ask that night. I noticed she removed her profile picture again. She only does this when she's stressed, not in the mood, or when something happens especially related to her ex. But I tried to call just to check on her. She didn't answer, but I knew she was resting because she had a cold.

Today is far from different from other days. Around 11am, Alhea messaged me — 'Gran stop na naten' I asked why. So I hurried up, packed my things at the office, and drove to her house. I asked her if it was a prank because it was already April 2. She said no. I met her and she told me everything that happened yesterday. My feeling was right — her ex rode the same jeep as her and they had an argument. Alhea asked me if I would hate her and not talk to her anymore, or if I would change because of that. I answered no. Why would I hate her? She didn't do anything wrong. I don't need to change anything because of that — it will be the same as before. I still like her, love her. I'm also willing to carry the weight and baggage of her trauma to make her happy and at peace. But I'm quite sure that I will never do what her ex did. I want to see her outgrow the traumas she has from her previous relationship. Everything that happens between us is a first for me, and I want us to grow together, overcome those things, and become better people and partners.

Nothing much happened this day. I went to work even though it was a holiday, just doing my usual work and stuff. I texted her to check how she was doing and if she was okay. I also said 'Praying for you. No matter what happens today, just keep moving forward. Maybe not today or tomorrow, but soon everything is going to be okay, okay?' And that was basically my day.

This was the day of the celebration for Tatay Henry, Alhea's stepfather. I brought pizza and coke. Later that evening I got to talk to her because I noticed that since the day before she wasn't okay. So we talked about how she was feeling. She said it's hard for her to move on. I also told her that if she really wants to move on, she needs to cut off certain people, not just her ex but also some family members, because every time she sees them the feelings come back. I also told her that if she still loves him, she should go back to him. I wouldn't get mad or anything but I would just set boundaries between us. I also shared a Japanese phrase with her, 'If you get on the wrong train, get off at the next station. The longer you stay on, the more expensive the return trip will be.' I confronted her about the fact that her ex is manipulative and gaslighting her. I told her that if she wants to go back, she should think about it carefully because going back into that situation is not a joke. I also told her that I was saying all of this not because of us, but because of her. I want her to make her own decision without me being a factor. Just her, choosing peace, choosing health and happiness. I also told her that I noticed she hasn't been happy and healthy lately. The conversation continued until I got home and called her. I added that I want her to grow, to see a better version of herself, and to overcome her traumas. I was thinking about her but she was also worried about us. She said she's happy with me and that I'm very different from him, that I'm too mature and she's not used to that. I also told her isn't it exciting and thrilling that I will court her again when she's free and trauma free. I offered that when she's ready I will court her again and we can start everything over. I made her cry because of all that. But I told her it's okay to cry, that I'm still here and I will listen even to her cry and her runny nose. We ended the call and I reminded her to make her decision carefully. I know it will take time but no matter what decision she makes I will respect it.

I arrived at the office early and Alhea was late. I noticed her seat was empty while doing technical support for the accounting department. She messaged me asking where to put the graham and salad she made because last Saturday she promised she would bring food for me. That made me smile. We had small conversations about that. Later in the afternoon we met in the pantry with her colleagues who are already aware of us. They asked a bunch of questions, about my loyalty, who I had courted in the office and such. I answered them nicely and jokingly. Later after office hours, I asked if I could walk her to the terminal but she said no since she was avoiding what happened last time. I understood that so instead I asked if I could call her. She said no. But I insisted and called anyway. She didn't pick up. So I apologized and suddenly she called back. She asked how my day was and I asked her how life has been for her lately. I'm so glad she's making progress. I told her I was so proud of her. She also mentioned that her supervisor talked to her about the same thing, about her ex and moving on and such. I was very very happy about that. I said my prayers are working because I have been praying for her a lot since everything started. We talked about how we began. From being strangers in the office, to her needing tech support, to transactions, our first burol date, the Christmas party practice, our first fight and resolution because I told her she was masungit and I didn't like that, our chats and date plans on January 2, 2026, how the date got postponed and moved to the following week, our unexpected meetup at an inuman invitation, my first confrontation about whether she still had emotional attachments, and our wedding during the Valentine's Day celebration in the office. I also asked if I could call her again tomorrow, just yes or no, no need to explain why. She said she wants to forget me already yet here I am still calling. She said she's getting used to it and will end up missing it.

I was late today because I overslept. I met Alhea in the pantry while going to get the salad she made for me. While we were there I told her I overslept and she was a bit shocked and laughed. Later I messaged her asking if she had eaten lunch but she hadn't. She was waiting for her supervisor to finish a meeting before they could eat. I even offered her coffee as a peace offering since I kept her up the night before. She said no and that she would make her own coffee. I said okay, next time then. I met her again in the pantry because I was looking for sugar. We don't have sugar in our office and drinking coffee without sugar is painful. We didn't talk much that time. Later I remembered I promised Easha, her niece, that I would get her a brooch similar to Alhea's so she could give the other half to her crush. So I went to look for Alhea and we bumped into each other at the corner as she was rushing to find her friend. I gave her the brooch and told her it was for Easha. I figured they were going out after office hours. As I was about to clock out I noticed her sitting by the window beside her friend Kyla. Kyla noticed me and laughed and called Alhea over. I waved goodbye at her. While in the parking lot I messaged her 'Si Kyla kasama mo?' and 'Ingat sa inyo po.' She replied 'yes' and 'Ingat ka din po.' Later that night I messaged her asking if I could call but I wasn't pressuring her since I knew she was tired. She messaged me that she wasn't home yet and sent pictures of where they were. I recognized the place, it was near the office. I told her 'Ingat kayo paguwi ha.'

Today was a very stressful day. I was overthinking whether I was being annoying to Alhea and because of that she thought I was mad at her. My mind was just messy from all the overthinking. So I told her about it and asked if I was being annoying. She said no. The funny thing is all my overthinking and worries disappeared the moment I talked to her. My motorcycle also had a flat tire today. Bad luck day. Haha. When I got home I told her that my overthinking and worries disappear whenever I talk to her. She also mentioned that she has made a decision. My gut is telling me the worst, that she decided to go back to her ex. But she didn't say anything yet, just my instincts. She said she will tell me next week, three days after my vacation. I told her that no matter what her decision is I will respect it. I will not get mad or anything. I told her these are the consequences of pursuing her and I need to face them. I also said that nothing will change but there will be boundaries to respect both her and me. I told her that whenever she needs a friend, a companion, someone to be with, or advice, not to hesitate to contact me. I will show up whenever she needs me. She cried a lot. She said I don't deserve her, that she doesn't deserve the love I'm giving her. I told her not to mind it and that whenever our paths cross again I hope that both of us are ready and free. I said my goodbye and see you next week on calls.

I'm writing this on April 13. During my vacation on the 10th and 11th, and on my way home on the 12th, I didn't do much. I was literally just overthinking what will happen to us after Alhea tells me her decision. During those days I was mentally absent from the vacation. I just slept, ate, laughed, drank, and slept again. It was heavy carrying all these thoughts. I kept asking myself, is Alhea also thinking about me? Do I ever cross her mind? Does she love me? She said the feelings were mutual but maybe I didn't give enough. Did I miss something? Is she really going back to her ex? Are they going out? Maybe I'm the problem? Does she like Gilbert? These thoughts never left my head until I fell asleep. I was literally crying every night. I felt like a fool for crying over something that felt like a fairytale. I felt too weak for this, too easily attached. Too many thoughts running through my mind. We met at the entrance today and that was the only interaction I had with her, just saying good morning. The days are becoming heavier especially when I get home and the same thoughts come back. I just want to fast forward time. I'm not writing this because I'm sad, but because these are the thoughts that have been running through my mind for the past few days.
